title: US sending Patriots to Ukraine under $1.85B aid package
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration announced Wednesday that it will provide $1.85 billion in military aid to Ukraine, rolling out funding for a Patriot missile battery as Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy arrived in Washington for his first known trip out of his country since Russia invaded in February.

title: Zelenskyy in US to meet Biden, address Congress as war rages
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy arrived in Washington on Wednesday for a summit with President Joe Biden and an address to Congress in a bid to shore up support for his country and send a defiant message to its Russian invaders.

title: Jan. 6 panel prepares to unveil final report on insurrection
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — An 800-page report set to be released by House investigators as soon as Wednesday will conclude that then-President Donald Trump criminally plotted to overturn his 2020 election defeat and “provoked his supporters to violence” at the Capitol with false claims of widespread voter fraud.

title: EXPLAINER: How Trump ignored advisers, spread election lies
shortDescription: The executive summary of the House Jan.

title: As flu rages, US releases medicine from national stockpile
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration said Wednesday it will release doses of prescription flu medicine from the Strategic National Stockpile to states as flu-sickened patients continue to flock to hospitals and doctors' offices around the country.

title: States contend with short timeline to correct broadband map
shortDescription: LOS ANGELES (AP) — States are racing against a deadline to challenge the map federal officials will use to divvy up the nation's largest-ever investment in high-speed internet.At stake is a share of the $42.5 billion Broadband Equity, Access and Deployment program, part of the infrastructure measure President Joe Biden signed into law last year.

title: The AP Interview: Vermont Sen. Leahy ponders his legacy
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — Sen. Patrick Leahy lingered on a narrow balcony on the west side of the U.S. Capitol, soaking in a panoramic view of the National Mall, the Washington Monument and, beyond, the Lincoln Memorial.

title: Lawmakers unveil $1.7T bill to avoid shutdown, boost Ukraine
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — Congressional leaders unveiled a government-wide $1.7 trillion spending package early Tuesday that includes another large round of aid to Ukraine, a nearly 10% boost in defense spending and roughly $40 billion in emergency spending, mostly to assist communities across the country recovering from drought, hurricanes and other natural disasters.

title: Arizona judge to consider Kari Lake’s stolen election claims
shortDescription: PHOENIX (AP) — Kari Lake has claimed for weeks that her loss in the race for Arizona governor was illegitimate.The former television anchor gets her long-sought opportunity to make her case to a judge this week during a two-day trial scheduled to begin on Wednesday.

title: Chief justice in Ohio map flap: Court attacks harm democracy
shortDescription: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — Ohio Chief Justice Maureen O'Connor isn't rattled on a personal level by the political attacks she endured from fellow Republicans during Ohio's protracted redistricting fight.

title: Trump tax audits required by IRS were delayed, panel says
Rule delay makes big EV tax credit possible early next year
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — People who want to buy an electric vehicle could get a bigger-than-expected tax credit come Jan. 1 because of a delay by the Treasury Department in drawing up rules for the tax breaks.

title: Peru Congress opens door to early elections amid unrest
shortDescription: LIMA, Peru (AP) — Peru’s Congress tentatively endorsed a plan on Tuesday to hold early elections in an attempt to defuse a national political crisis marked by deadly unrest after lawmakers ousted President Pedro Castillo.

title: Kentucky GOP Rep. Maddox drops out of 2023 governor’s race
shortDescription: LOUISVILLE, Ky. (AP) — Kentucky state Rep. Savannah Maddox has dropped out of a crowded primary field of Republicans hoping to topple Democratic Gov. Andy Beshear in next year's election. In a news release, Maddox said it became clear she would not have the resources needed to succeed despite extensive fundraising efforts.

title: Writer says her love life stopped after Trump raped her
shortDescription: NEW YORK (AP) — The advice columnist E. Jean Carroll said during a deposition that the “music had stopped” in her love life after she was raped by Donald Trump in the 1990s at an upscale Manhattan department store.

title: Ethics board dismisses Noem plane case, says law is unclear
shortDescription: SIOUX FALLS, S.D. (AP) — South Dakota's ethics board on Tuesday dismissed a complaint against Gov. Kristi Noem for using state government aircraft to attend events hosted by political organizations because state law does not define what is meant by “state business.”

title: County vote moves Pennsylvania closer to certifying election
shortDescription: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — A suburban Philadelphia county elections board voted Tuesday to certify its November results, a development Pennsylvania officials had said was required before they can issue a statewide certification.

title: Congress moves to ban TikTok from US government devices
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — TikTok would be banned from most U.S. government devices under a spending bill Congress unveiled early Tuesday, the latest push by American lawmakers against the Chinese-owned social media app.

title: Spain: Top court puts brakes on govt in unprecedented move
shortDescription: MADRID (AP) — Spain’s top court has handed down a ruling preventing the upper house of Parliament from debating and voting on an amendment to the country's penal code — an unprecedented interference in the legislature that foreshadows potential political turmoil for the leftist coalition government heading into election year.

title: Analysis: Musk and Trump, 2 disrupters face reckoning
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — Elon Musk and Donald Trump share bestride-the-colossus egos, an incessant desire to be the center of attention and a platform to showcase their eccentricities and erraticism.Both the Tesla CEO and the former president have used that platform, Twitter, as a sword and a shield — a soapbox to rouse the passions (and tap the pocketbooks) of tens of millions of followers and repulse the other side.

title: How Congress is changing electoral law in response to Jan. 6
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — In one of the last acts of the Democratic-led Congress, the House and the Senate are set to pass an overhaul of the Electoral Count Act, the arcane election law that then-President Donald Trump tried to subvert after his 2020 election defeat.

title: Gambling interests gave GOP group behind pro-DeWine PACs $1M
shortDescription: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — Gambling interests positioning for lucrative business as Ohio remakes its betting landscape donated nearly $1 million to a nonprofit group that helped successfully reelect Republican Ohio Gov.

title: Biden heads to Mexico next month for leaders summit
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden will travel to Mexico next month for North American leaders summit, White House officials said Tuesday.

title: GOP’s usual embrace of Trump muted after criminal referral
Tunisia judge orders opposition top official’s imprisonment
shortDescription: TUNIS, Tunisia (AP) — An anti-terrorism judge in Tunisia has ordered the imprisonment of former Prime Minister Ali Larayedh, vice-president of the popular Islamist opposition party Ennahdha, days after the country held a legislative election marked by a very low turnout.

title: Rabuka promises change after winning close Fiji election
shortDescription: WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — Sitiveni Rabuka has won a close election in Fiji and is promising change as the South Pacific nation's first new leader in 16 years.

title: Kari Lake will get to make case for election misconduct
shortDescription: PHOENIX (AP) — A judge on Monday dismissed part of a lawsuit filed by Kari Lake, the defeated Republican candidate for Arizona governor, but will allow her to call witnesses in an attempt to prove that she lost because of misconduct by election officials.

title: At Hanukkah event, Biden condemns ‘venom’ of antisemitism
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden on Monday expressed alarm about growing antisemitism in the United States and around the globe and vowed to fight back against the scourge.Speaking to guests gathered for a Hanukkah reception at the White House, Biden said “silence is complicity,” and added that it’s imperative that hate, violence and antisemitism are condemned by the nation.

title: US homeless numbers stay about the same as before pandemic
shortDescription: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — President Joe Biden's administration announced Monday that it is ramping up efforts to help house people now sleeping on sidewalks and in tents and cars as a new federal report confirms what's obvious to people in many cities: Homelessness is persisting despite increased local efforts.

title: Democrats propose assault weapon ban, gun purchase permit
shortDescription: TUKWILA, Wash. (AP) — Washington Gov. Jay Inslee and Attorney General Bob Ferguson announced legislative proposals on Monday that would prohibit assault weapons, require a permit to purchase a gun and make gun sellers potentially liable for negligent sales in the state.

title: Biden: US looking to strengthen relationship with Ecuador
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — With Ecuadorian President Guillermo Lasso by his side, President Joe Biden said Monday the U.S. is looking to expand and strengthen the U.S.

title: Notable dignitaries who have addressed Congress
shortDescription: Visiting foreign leaders address Joint Meetings of Congress often during state visits to the United States.

title: Ukrainian President Zelenskyy to meet with Biden, address Congress
shortDescription: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy will make his first trip to Washington since Russia began its violent invasion of Ukraine in February.

title: Supreme Court denies Trump effort to keep tax returns from House panel
shortDescription: Trump's record at the Supreme Court is not particularly good, even though he nominated three of the court's current associate justices.

title: House panel votes to release Trump tax information
shortDescription: The House Ways and Means Committee got copies of Donald Trump's tax returns from the Treasury Department a few weeks ago after a long legal battle.

title: House panel votes on Trump's tax returns
shortDescription: The Democrat-led House Ways and Means Committee voted to release some of former President Donald Trump's tax returns in the next few days.

title: Will Hope Hicks' complete Jan. 6 testimony be damning for Trump?
shortDescription: The Jan. 6 committee revealed damaging testimony from Trump loyalist Hope Hicks on Monday. With full report and transcripts coming, does it have more?

title: Biden urges Supreme Court to let it end Title 42 migrant program
shortDescription: Biden's response came a day after Chief Justice Roberts temporarily blocked the Biden administration from ending Title 42 migrant expulsions.

title: OnPolitics: Will Donald Trump's taxes become public?
shortDescription: After years of speculation and legal action, the public may soon get a look at some long-sought information: Donald Trump's tax records.

title: Lawmakers unveil bill to avoid government shutdown, boost Ukraine
shortDescription: Congressional leaders unveiled a $1.7 trillion spending package early Tuesday that includes another large round of aid to Ukraine, a nearly 10% boost in defense spending.

title: Biden to travel to Mexico for meeting with AMLO amid migrant crisis
shortDescription: President Joe Biden's announced visit comes amid a surge in migrants that has taxed border communities and as the U.S. weighs lifting Title 42.

title: Pence says he doesn't think Trump should face criminal charges over Jan. 6
shortDescription: Former Vice President Mike Pence said Donald Trump acted recklessly, but not criminally, on Jan. 6 so should not be charged by the Justice Department.

title: Election law overhaul part of spending bill before Congress
shortDescription: An overhaul of the 1887 Electoral Count Act was included in the spending bill Congress will debate this week.

title: Will Trump's taxes become public? What to expect if House panel votes for release.
shortDescription: The House Committee on Ways and Means meets Tuesday to decide what to do with Trump tax records it obtained after years of legal action.

title: The Jan. 6 report: What remains unknown in the Capitol attack?
shortDescription: The Jan. 6 committee's report Monday left out details on potential witness tampering and a reported clash between Donald Trump and the Secret Service.

title: Unprecedented: Jan. 6 panel votes criminal referral for Trump
shortDescription: After a million documents and a thousand interviews, the committee concludes Trump bears responsibility for the mob and their assault.

title: Supreme Court pauses Biden effort to end Title 42 immigration policy
shortDescription: Title 42 has been used to expel migrants more than 2.4 million times since 2020. GOP-led states want to require the Biden administration to continue.

title: Trump criminal referrals: Inciting insurrection could be hardest to prove
shortDescription: Jan. 6 committee members pointed to Trump's provocative address on the Ellipse when he took no action during the next three hours to stop the assault.

title: Jan. 6 panel urges DOJ charge Trump over efforts to undo 2020 election: recap
shortDescription: Congress' 18-month investigation into former President Donald Trump's role in the Jan. 6, 2021 U.S. Capitol attack will come to a close this week.

title: OnPolitics: Jan. 6 committee urges DOJ charge Trump in Capitol attack
shortDescription: The House panel on Jan. 6 recommends the Justice Department prosecute Donald Trump for his efforts to overturn the 2020 election in its final report.

title: Jan. 6 report finds Donald Trump 'central cause' of Capitol attack
shortDescription: The House committee investigating the Capitol attack on Jan. 6, 2021, labeled Donald Trump the 'central cause' of the riot.

title: States ask Supreme Court to keep Trump-era Title 42 migrant policy
shortDescription: President Biden is trying to unwind a Trump-era policy that permits the rapid expulsion of migrants. Critics want the Supreme Court to stop him.

title: Congress and Industry Leaders Call for Crackdown on Hospice Fraud
shortDescription: Following a ProPublica-New Yorker investigation into the hospice industry, members of the Comprehensive Care Caucus and national trade groups are demanding reform.

title: Congress Passes Bill to Rein in Conflicts of Interest for Consultants Such as McKinsey
shortDescription: President Joe Biden is expected to sign the legislation, whose Senate sponsors cited ProPublica’s reporting on McKinsey’s conflicts in working for both the FDA and opioid makers like Purdue Pharma.

title: Why Congress Can’t Stop the CIA From Working With Forces That Commit Abuses
shortDescription: The Leahy Law prohibits the U.S. military from providing training and equipment to foreign security forces that commit human rights abuses, but it does not apply to U.S. intelligence agencies. Vermont Sen. Patrick Leahy said it should.

title: What Really Saved the Democrats This Year?
shortDescription: Progressives and moderates both have a theory of the case. By Thomas B. Edsall

title: Zelensky Will Meet With Biden in Washington and Address Congress, Officials Say
shortDescription: The visit will be the first time President Volodymyr Zelensky of Ukraine has left his country since Russia invaded in February.By Michael D. Shear and Emily Cochrane

title: In Virginia, Democrats Sprint to Select Nominee for Special House Election
shortDescription: After a congressman’s death last month, Jennifer McClellan, a state senator who lost a bid for governor in 2021, is the front-runner and could become the first Black woman to represent Virginia in Congress.By Maya King

title: Lawmakers Advance Sprawling Spending Bill in Race to Avoid Shutdown
shortDescription: The legislation, which would fund the government through September, would significantly increase federal spending and provide billions of dollars in emergency aid.By Emily Cochrane

title: A Diminished Trump Meets a Damning Narrative

shortDescription: From the lobster industry to TikTok to telehealth, the massive year-end government funding packages casts a wide net across public policy.
